quote: The future is already here — it’s just not evenly distributed.
through_line: AI is reshaping both the economics of attention and the toolset of those building for it. Before DMOs can adapt their strategy, they need a clear-eyed view of what’s changing — and what it means that the skills we teach children today will shape who gets to navigate that change.

articles.title: The State of AI: the economic singularity
articles.pitch: MIT Technology Review’s annual State of AI report reframes the moment we’re living through: not a disruption, but a singularity — a point after which economic life won’t return to its previous shape. A dense but essential read for anyone trying to understand what the next few years of work actually look like.

articles.title: What Destination Website Performance Taught Us in Q1 2025: Organic Trends, AI Impact, and the Road Ahead
articles.pitch: Organic traffic to destination websites fell in Q1 2025, and the cause isn’t a mystery: AI-powered search is rerouting how travelers discover places. Tempest’s Q1 data lays out the shift in concrete terms — the kind of numbers that should inform every content and SEO decision DMOs make this year.

articles.title: AI Search Is Transforming Tourism Marketing
articles.pitch: Search is no longer about keywords — it’s about being the answer. Tourism Tribe maps how AI search is changing what destination content needs to do, why traditional SEO thinking is losing ground, and what tourism marketers should build toward instead.

articles.title: Why kids still need to learn to code in the age of AI
articles.pitch: Five arguments for why the answer is still yes, even as AI tools generate increasingly sophisticated code. The Raspberry Pi Foundation doesn’t hedge: coding is a literacy, not a job skill, and the children who develop it will have agency over a world the rest of us will only consume.

articles.title: A Mind Like Sky
articles.pitch: Kornfield offers one of his most essential teachings: awareness is not a state to achieve but a sky that’s always already present. A short, gentle read to begin the week with — not a technique, but a reminder of what was here before attention narrowed.

articles.title: Crafting Generative CSS Worlds
articles.pitch: What happens when you treat CSS as a canvas rather than a constraint? This tutorial builds generative art systems entirely in CSS — animated, infinite, genuinely beautiful. A reminder that the web is still a place where you can make something you couldn’t have predicted.
